mnemonics: PQ Max: A Heap's 'Peek' is Quick (O(1)), but 'Extract-Max' (O(log n)) requires fixing the heap structure.
keyPoints: A heap is a complete binary tree that satisfies the heap property (max-heap: parent >= children, min-heap: parent <= children).
Heapsort uses a max-heap to sort an array by repeatedly extracting the maximum element and placing it at the end.
The BUILD-MAX-HEAP procedure runs in O(n) time, not O(n log n), because most nodes are near the bottom of the tree.
commonMistakes: Forgetting that arrays are 0-indexed, leading to incorrect parent/child index calculations (e.g., parent(i) = floor((i-1)/2)).
Assuming the entire heapsort algorithm runs in O(n) time, when its overall complexity is O(n log n).
